conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm an AI mental health assistant — everything we discuss here is completely confidential, so please feel comfortable. How's your week been, getting close to Thanksgiving?
Oh, an AI, huh. Alright. And it's confidential — fine, okay. Traffic was fine.
That's great! It can be a busy time of year. Can you tell me a bit about what your daily routine looks like these days? What are some things you're prioritizing?
Well, school's been keeping me pretty busy. I'm trying to get my grades up so I can finish my degree.
That sounds like a challenging but rewarding goal. How's your work at the college student union going?
It's okay, I guess. I mean, it's not my dream job or anything, but it pays the bills and gives me some structure.
That makes sense. What kind of social life do you have outside of school and work? Do you have any close friends or family that you're making time for?
Honestly, it's been a bit tough to find time for that lately. I've just been so exhausted all the time.
Yeah, exhaustion can be really overwhelming. Can you tell me more about what your sleep patterns have been like? Are you sleeping okay?
Sleep? Oh, no. I mean, I sleep fine usually, but sometimes I'll wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there for hours.
That sounds really tough. What are some things that you're doing to try to improve your energy levels?
Honestly, I don't know. I just feel so burnt out all the time. And then when I'm tired, I just want to stay in bed and not do anything.
It can be hard to muster up the motivation to do things when you're feeling exhausted. Are there any activities or hobbies that you used to enjoy doing but haven't had the energy for lately?
Yeah, I used to love hiking, but now it's just too much effort.
That sounds really challenging. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or eating habits recently? Eating less or more than usual?
Oh yeah, definitely. I've been skipping meals left and right because I just don't have the energy to cook or anything.
It can be hard to prioritize self-care when you're feeling so drained. What are some things that you do in your daily routine that bring you joy or relaxation?
Honestly? Nothing, really. I mean, sometimes I'll watch TV before bed if I'm tired enough, but that's about it.
That sounds like a tough place to be stuck. Have you noticed any changes in your mood recently? Feeling more irritable or anxious?
Yeah, all the time. Like, little things will set me off and I'll just get so worked up over nothing.
It can be really helpful to identify those triggers and work on managing them. What are some things that you do when you're feeling overwhelmed like that?
Honestly? Nothing usually. I just kind of... shut down, I guess.

It sounds like you might be struggling with feelings of guilt or shame around not being able to cope with those emotions. Can you tell me more about what's been going on that's making you feel so guilty?
Oh, just the usual stuff – missing deadlines, not meeting expectations... I don't know, it feels like everything is falling apart and I'm just stuck.
That sounds really tough. It can be helpful to challenge those negative thought patterns and focus on what you're doing well. What are some things that you're proud of accomplishing lately?
Honestly? Not much, unfortunately. But I did finish a project at work recently, so that was something.
That's great! Congratulations on completing that project. It can be really empowering to achieve goals like that. What are your hopes for our time together today?
I guess just to... figure out some ways to deal with all this stuff and maybe find a way to make my life feel more manageable.
That sounds like a great place to start. Let's work together to get you feeling more grounded and in control. Is there anything specific that you're hoping to achieve by the end of our time together?
Yeah, I guess just to have some tools or strategies to help me cope with everything.
We can definitely work on that. Let's start by exploring some ways to improve your sleep habits. Can you tell me more about what your daily routine looks like in terms of sleep and wake-up time?
Okay... I usually go to bed pretty late, around 2 or 3 am, and then I'll wake up whenever I do.
And how's that working out for you? Are you getting the restful sleep that you need?
No, definitely not. Like, sometimes I'll fall asleep during the day and then stay in bed for hours.
It sounds like we might want to explore some strategies for establishing a more consistent sleep schedule. Would that be something you'd be interested in working on?
Yeah, okay. That might help.
